rag_rules: Fix unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations in AGREE annexes by tracing root causes or removing invalid clauses.
Place component type assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes, avoiding implementation-level bindings in type contracts.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers within AGREE annexes to avoid type errors.
Ensure AGREE contracts reference valid identifiers and avoid referencing non-existent packages or property sets.
When placing assumptions or guarantees, ensure they are scoped to the appropriate component type or implementation level.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes must reference visible identifiers; avoid using undefined or non-existent features.
Guarantee expressions must have valid types; unresolved identifiers lead to <error> type issues.
anti_patterns: Do not add architecture elements to fix AGREE type errors; instead, resolve root causes or remove invalid clauses.
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type contracts; use appropriate scopes.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about package or property set visibility
